title: COPD exacerbation follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

referral.text: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.
referral.rationale: Supports structured COPD follow-up and review for COPD exacerbation follow-up; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

source.title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.137–1.2.140 and Table 6 — follow-up and review

follow_up.text: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.
follow_up.rationale: Supports COPD exacerbation symptom features for COPD exacerbation follow-up; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Exacerbation date and severity, treatment, recovery, symptoms, oxygenation, inhalers, adherence, technique, smoking, examination, tests and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for COPD exacerbation follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
